drafts.title: Curator 'update over create' over-aggressively maps topic-related to update
drafts.compiledTruth: The sediment curator's 'update over create' principle (ADR 0016) is over-aggressive in LLM-driven practice: any candidate touching a related TOPIC gets interpreted as an update to an existing entry, and the update instruction 'new current best truth, not append-only delta' gets read as WHOLESALE OVERWRITE rather than INTEGRATE delta. Two root causes compound: (1) literal execution of update-over-create (same topic area treated as update target, not distinguishing refines-same-claim vs downstream-observation); (2) over-reading 'best truth not delta' (candidate compiled_truth replaces the entry body instead of being integrated into it). Result: P0 data loss.

drafts.title: P0: curator replaced retrieval-anchor trigger_phrases wholesale (521405b)
drafts.compiledTruth: Real incident (abrain commit 521405b): updating entry 'sediment-architecture-shift-from-gate-heavy-extractor-to-llm-curator', the curator REPLACED all 6 trigger_phrases (e.g. 'bidirectional gate', 'input gate', 'output gate') with 4 unrelated phrases describing the current session's new topic. The old phrases were the entry's core retrieval anchors; after replacement memory_search 'bidirectional gate' no longer hits the entry. Lesson: trigger_phrases are retrieval anchors; new session topics must not evict old ones.

drafts.title: P0: curator overwrote load-bearing sections treating downstream obs as update (2e8924d)
drafts.compiledTruth: Real incident (abrain commit 2e8924d): entry 'sanitizer-credential-patterns-are-perpetually-incomplete' (45 lines, H2 sections Evidence/Fix/Principle) was overwritten to 27 lines with title renamed and ALL three load-bearing H2 sections deleted. The candidate actually described a NEW downstream operational hazard (assistant echoing fixture credential strings causing self-fulfilling abort cycle); it should have been CREATE with derives_from:[sanitizer-credential-patterns-are-perpetually-incomplete], never an update of the upstream pattern. Only git history could recover the lost evidence/fix/principle.
drafts.sourceHeading: 实证 2：load-bearing sections 整段覆盖

drafts.title: Two P0s of one root cause equals systematic problem, not coincidence
drafts.compiledTruth: Two curator data-loss P0s within 48h of B5 sediment writer cutover shared one root cause with two faces. Sample size = 2 is sufficient to declare a systematic problem. The first P0 (trigger_phrases swap) was subtle and easy to dismiss; the second (body overwrite) exposed the true severity. Had the first strange single observation been taken seriously and investigated immediately, the second could have been prevented. Maxim: a single strange observation in a self-evolving system deserves serious tracking, not dismissal.

drafts.title: UPDATE only refines the same claim; downstream observation is CREATE with derives_from
drafts.compiledTruth: Curator discipline (Layer 1 prompt, retained): Use UPDATE only when the candidate REFINES THE SAME claim the neighbor already makes (corrects an error, adds confidence, narrows scope, supplies a better compiled truth for the SAME assertion). Use CREATE with derives_from:[<neighbor-slug>] when the candidate is a DOWNSTREAM observation building on the neighbor's premise but stating a DIFFERENT claim (new failure mode, new hazard, new consequence, new specialization). 'Same topic area' is NOT grounds for update. When in doubt prefer CREATE with derives_from: a spurious duplicate is recoverable via later merge; an UPDATE overwriting durable evidence/fix/principle is data loss recoverable only via git.

drafts.title: On update, preserve load-bearing sections verbatim; compiled_truth is a delta to integrate
drafts.compiledTruth: Update body-preservation contract (Layer 1 prompt, retained): when updating, compiled_truth is the new current best truth, BUT preserve the neighbor's Evidence/Fix/Principle/code-example and similarly load-bearing sections VERBATIM unless the candidate explicitly contradicts a specific sentence. Removing such a section just because the candidate no longer discusses it is THE bug. Treat the candidate's compiled_truth as a DELTA proposal to integrate into the existing body, not a replacement body. The candidate's TITLE is a hint, not a directive; a different-claim title is a strong signal to CREATE, not UPDATE.

drafts.title: trigger_phrases on update must UNION not REPLACE (writer-enforced)
drafts.compiledTruth: trigger_phrases UNION semantics (Layer 1 prompt + Layer 2 writer enforcement, both retained): on update, UNION existing trigger_phrases with the candidate's, never REPLACE. Drop an existing phrase only if the candidate explicitly retires that sub-claim; otherwise keep all old phrases (they are retrieval anchors and losing them breaks future memory_search). Wanting to fully replace trigger_phrases almost always means you meant CREATE. The writer mechanically enforces the union (curator cannot bypass), with case-insensitive dedup and scalar-string compatibility as defense-in-depth against silent drop. This is data enrichment, not a blocking reject gate.

drafts.title: Silent-reject mechanical gates create unrepairable dead entries
drafts.compiledTruth: The body_shrink and body_section_loss mechanical reject gates (shipped in 07e4a3e) were deliberately REMOVED in ee1c809. Reason: a silent reject creates an unrepairable dead entry; the curator keeps retrying the same operation, keeps getting rejected, nobody is notified, and the entry rots permanently. This violates sediment's self-evolution philosophy. A silent-reject gate that blocks writes is MORE harmful than a single LLM knowledge degradation, because the degradation is self-healable by later iterations while the dead entry is not. Anti-pattern: blocking mechanical guards with no audit-consumer / alert / human-notify side.

drafts.title: body_shrink byte gate (removed): under 50% of old length, missed 14%-shrink section loss
drafts.compiledTruth: Historical mechanism (07e4a3e, removed ee1c809): the body_shrink reject fired when an update's new compiledTruth byte length was under 50% of the old. Threshold 0.5 chosen because normal trim of stale sentences is usually under 20% and aggressive consolidation rarely halves; 0.5 was loose enough to allow legit refactor, tight enough to catch byte-level outright overwrite. Critical limitation: byte-length alone could NOT catch the 2e8924d class (that case shrank only 14% in bytes yet deleted 3 H2 sections). This is why a structural check was needed to complement it. Lesson: byte-length is a poor proxy for semantic loss.

drafts.title: body_section_loss H2-retention gate (removed): retain at least ceil(old/2) H2 sections
drafts.compiledTruth: Historical mechanism (07e4a3e, removed ee1c809): the body_section_loss reject fired when the old body had at least 2 H2 sections and the new body retained fewer than ceil(oldCount/2) of the original H2 headings. Example: old 4 sections required at least 2 retained; keeping only 1 triggers reject. This structural check caught the 2e8924d class (small byte shrink but wholesale section loss) that byte-length could not. Demonstrates structural/H2-retention checks beat byte-count for detecting semantic deletion, but it too was removed under the trust-the-LLM retreat.

drafts.title: Shared mutation API guards must branch on op semantics (merge allows shrink)
drafts.compiledTruth: One mergeUpdateMarkdown serves update/archive/supersede/merge/soft-delete, each with DIFFERENT invariants: merge synthesizes a body legitimately shorter than each source (consolidation), update must not shrink, archive/supersede/soft-delete don't pass compiledTruth at all. A guard that ignores op context mis-fires; opus's review caught the body_shrink gate killing the legitimate merge path. Fix at the time was skipBodyShrinkGuard opt-in only for mergeProjectEntries, while archive/supersede skip naturally because they omit compiledTruth. Maxim: a guard on a multi-op mutation API must read op context or expose an explicit opt-in bypass, never apply one op's invariant to all.

drafts.title: Smoke regression fixtures lock retained curator-defense invariants
drafts.compiledTruth: Defense behaviors were locked with smoke regression fixtures so future refactors go red on breakage. After ee1c809 the retained fixtures cover: trigger_phrases UNION (create + update + legacy scalar form, with original-casing wins and case-insensitive dup drop), curator prompt marker assertions, and sanitizer defenses. The body_shrink / body_section_loss fixtures were removed alongside their gates. Pattern: encode each defense invariant as a smoke fixture, including the negative cases (e.g. 'preserve at least 50% body still updates successfully' to prove the guard doesn't kill legit refactors).

drafts.title: Dual-model review surfaced complementary P1/P2 defects before ship
drafts.compiledTruth: Dual-model review of this defense ship found complementary defects. opus (130s): two P1s - merge path would be falsely killed by body_shrink because merge necessarily shrinks (fix: skipBodyShrinkGuard bypass); and a comment claiming to catch 2e8924d that the byte-length threshold actually couldn't (added the section-loss floor, comment corrected). deepseek (177s): one P2 - scalar-string trigger_phrases silently dropped (scalar-compat branch); plus verified all 5 writer mutation paths handle the guard correctly. Both models GO. Lesson: independent reviewers catch decorrelated failure modes.
drafts.sourceHeading: 双模型 review 关键发现

drafts.title: Don't configize body_shrink threshold - users would disable the floor
drafts.compiledTruth: Decision (while the gate still existed): do NOT make the body_shrink threshold (0.5) configurable. Configurability would let users in autoLlmWriteEnabled scenarios accidentally lower it toward 0, effectively disabling the defense. If a legitimate over-50% shrink use-case ever arises, the correct path is supersede/archive/delete, not loosening the floor. General principle: don't expose a safety-floor knob that a careless config can zero out.

drafts.title: Phrase retirement must be an explicit list, never inferred from omission
drafts.compiledTruth: Decision: with writer-enforced UNION, a single trigger_phrase cannot be retired via the curator omitting it; that path is intentionally closed (omission does not equal intent to delete). Whole-entry retirement goes through supersede/archive; the only current way to retire one phrase is manual git edit, a known limitation. If real need arises, add an explicit patch.triggerPhrasesRetire:string[] field where the curator must actively LIST phrases to drop. Principle: destructive intent must be explicit, never inferred from absence.

drafts.title: Don't build create-despite-neighbors monitoring before an audit consumer exists
drafts.compiledTruth: Decision: do NOT auto-monitor create-despite-neighbors counts yet. opus flagged that the new 'when in doubt prefer CREATE' prompt could breed duplicate proliferation, but there is currently no audit consumer (no alert threshold, no sediment self-improve hook). Build the monitor only after actually observing a duplicate family (at least 3 entries with derives_from sharing one ancestor). Principle: don't add monitoring with no consumer; wait for evidence of the problem.

drafts.title: abrain git history is the ultimate sediment data-loss rollback surface
drafts.compiledTruth: abrain is a git repo, so git history is the ultimate rollback surface for sediment data loss; revert is always the first recovery move. The commit-message convention 'sediment: <op> <slug> (project:<id>)' makes losses easy to grep and trace. The damaged P0 entry was restored via git checkout HEAD~1 (f066279). When a guard rejects a dangerous update, nothing is written to disk (atomicWrite never runs), further lowering recovery cost. Maxim: lean on git as the recovery layer rather than trying to make every write irreversible-safe.

drafts.title: Retreat from mechanical gates to trusting LLM curator self-evolution
drafts.compiledTruth: Precedent: after shipping three mechanical defense layers against curator data loss, the team RETREATED, removing the blocking gates (body_shrink, body_section_loss) and keeping only prompt discipline + trigger_phrases UNION enrichment + sanitizer hard boundary. Revised lesson (ee1c809): a single LLM knowledge degradation is self-healable by later iterations, but a silent-reject mechanical gate produces an unrepairable dead entry, so trusting the LLM curator's self-correction beats hard blocking. Keep only non-blocking safe boundaries (sanitizer as hard limit, UNION as enrichment). This is the canonical AI-Native experience chain: prefer prompt engineering over mechanical guards.
